2026-03-21T09:14:02.122Z DEBUG SQL: INSERT INTO "orders" ("id","user_id","account_id","status","created_at") VALUES ($1,$2,$3,$4,$5) RETURNING "id","user_id","account_id";
2026-03-21T09:14:02.122Z DEBUG replacements=["9d6a0d5d-6c52-4c77-bc4b-2f0a2c6f7b63","8f9e5c0b-1e0a-4c44-a4e0-6bc4b29f0b91","18442","PENDING","2026-03-21T09:14:02.117Z"]
2026-03-21T09:14:02.123Z INFO  user-context req=7fd2b1c4-2a81-4c3e-b1ef-9d4f2a1e9d8a auth.sub=18442 auth.email=[ops+test@acme.io](mailto:ops+test@acme.io) auth.role=admin
2026-03-21T09:14:02.124Z WARN  mapper accountId missing from JWT claims, falling back to auth.sub
2026-03-21T09:14:02.124Z ERROR POST /v1/orders 500 184ms - msg="db insert failed"

output.issue: Order creation is failing because a numeric user identifier is being written into a PostgreSQL UUID column.
output.root_cause: The service maps account_id from JWT claims incorrectly. When accountId is missing, the code falls back to auth.sub, which in this request is the numeric string "18442". The orders.account_id column expects a UUID, so PostgreSQL rejects the insert with error 22P02.
output.fix: Stop using auth.sub as a fallback for account_id unless it is validated as a UUID. Update the JWT-to-model mapper in the order service so account_id comes only from the proper accountId claim or a database lookup. Add schema-level/request validation before the INSERT, for example reject non-UUID account_id values with a 400 response, and backfill or fix any clients still sending tokens without accountId.

output.issue: Celery billing job crashes before charging because a TTL environment variable is misformatted.
output.root_cause: PAYMENT_LOCK_TTL_SECONDS is set to the string "30s" but the Python code casts it directly with int(...), which only accepts a bare integer like "30".
output.fix: Change PAYMENT_LOCK_TTL_SECONDS to a numeric value such as 30 in the deployment config or secrets. Also harden the code to parse duration suffixes safely or fail fast during app startup with config validation.

2026-03-21T05:07:54.745Z DEBUG [com.acme.checkout.repo.CartItemRepo] /* insert com.acme.checkout.model.CartItem */ insert into cart_item (cart_id,qty,sku,updated_at,id) values (?,?,?,?,?)
2026-03-21T05:07:54.746Z INFO  [checkout-api] reqId=1bf4b44f-0ecf-4fef-98d8-0c812e48e1f0 status=500 durationMs=15

output.issue: Cart merge is failing on a unique constraint when the same SKU exists in both carts.
output.root_cause: The merge flow blindly inserts guest cart items into the user's cart instead of checking whether the target cart already contains the same SKU and updating quantity.
output.fix: Change the merge logic to upsert by (cart_id, sku): fetch existing item and increment qty, or use a database upsert such as INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (cart_id, sku) DO UPDATE SET qty = cart_item.qty + EXCLUDED.qty.

2026/03/21 14:11:14 ERROR run failed after retries err="Get [https://ledger.internal.svc.cluster.local/v1/settlements?date=2026-03-20](https://ledger.internal.svc.cluster.local/v1/settlements?date=2026-03-20): tls: failed to verify certificate: x509: certificate is valid for ledger.prod.svc.cluster.local, ledger, not ledger.internal.svc.cluster.local"

output.issue: Go service cannot call the ledger API because TLS hostname verification fails.
output.root_cause: The client is connecting to ledger.internal.svc.cluster.local, but the server certificate SANs only cover ledger.prod.svc.cluster.local and ledger. The requested hostname does not match the certificate.
output.fix: Update the service endpoint to use a hostname present in the certificate, or reissue the ledger service certificate with ledger.internal.svc.cluster.local in its SAN list. Do not disable TLS verification.

instruction: Analyze the following logs and identify the issue.
input:
2026-03-21T08:59:11.907Z server[web.1]: incoming POST /hooks/github delivery=9b0ea620-4af1-11ef-8b69-a19cda2fef99 ua=GitHub-Hookshot/5876d1d
2026-03-21T08:59:11.908Z server[web.1]: body bytes=1843 sig256=sha256=3a9d0f...ab17 event=push
2026-03-21T08:59:11.910Z server[web.1]: ERROR webhook verification failed
RangeError [ERR_CRYPTO_TIMING_SAFE_EQUAL_LENGTH]: Input buffers must have the same byte length
    at new NodeError (node:internal/errors:405:5)
    at Object.timingSafeEqual (node:crypto:433:11)
    at verifySignature (/app/src/lib/githubWebhook.js:27:17)
    at processWebhook (/app/src/routes/github.js:61:21)
    at Layer.handle [as handle_request] (/app/node_modules/express/lib/router/layer.js:95:5)
    at next (/app/node_modules/express/lib/router/route.js:149:13)
    at Route.dispatch (/app/node_modules/express/lib/router/route.js:119:3)
    at Layer.handle [as handle_request] (/app/node_modules/express/lib/router/layer.js:95:5)
2026-03-21T08:59:11.911Z server[web.1]: DEBUG expectedSig=sha256=7f9328f28b4f8b6ac6f4f31c4a29d2b1d5a8aebc53a79cfa1d7efeb0b3d4c771
2026-03-21T08:59:11.911Z server[web.1]: DEBUG receivedSig=3a9d0f...ab17
2026-03-21T08:59:11.911Z server[web.1]: WARN header x-hub-signature-256 missing prefix? raw=3a9d0f...ab17
2026-03-21T08:59:11.913Z server[web.1]: status=500 duration_ms=6

output.issue: GitHub webhook verification crashes because signature strings are compared with different buffer lengths.
output.root_cause: The incoming x-hub-signature-256 header is malformed or stripped of the required 'sha256=' prefix, but the code still passes the expected and received values directly into crypto.timingSafeEqual without normalizing and length-checking them first.
output.fix: Validate that x-hub-signature-256 is present and starts with 'sha256='. Parse both signatures into equal-length raw byte buffers before calling timingSafeEqual. If the header is invalid, return 401 instead of throwing.

output.issue: Rails CSV import crashes when a required column value is blank.
output.root_cause: user_csv_importer.rb calls strip on a nil field value from the parsed CSV row, likely because a required column such as email or name is missing in one of the rows.
output.fix: Add nil-safe parsing and validation in parse_row, for example use row['email']&.strip and reject rows with missing required fields with a clear row-level validation error instead of raising a 500.

output.issue: Shipment creation fails because the tracking number exceeds the database column length.
output.root_cause: The TrackingNumber value '1Z999AA10123456784-RETURN' is longer than the SQL Server column definition for Shipment.TrackingNumber, causing SQL error 2628 during insert.

output.issue: Rust image worker crashes because the pod's ephemeral storage is full.
output.root_cause: The worker writes temp thumbnails into /tmp and unwraps the write result. The node or container has exhausted ephemeral storage, producing OS error 28 and causing a panic.
output.fix: Free or increase ephemeral storage for the pod/node, reduce temp/cache usage, and stop using unwrap on file writes. Handle storage errors gracefully and configure a bounded cache or mount a larger persistent volume for temp image processing.

2026-03-21T16:03:17.443Z INFO  auth-service reqId=5b26bb04f5d94ab3 status=302 location=/dashboard

output.issue: OAuth callback fails with 502 because the upstream response headers exceed Nginx's buffer limits.
output.root_cause: The auth service returns multiple large Set-Cookie headers totaling about 11 KB, including an oversized id_token cookie. Nginx rejects the upstream response with 'upstream sent too big header'.
output.fix: Stop storing large tokens directly in cookies, especially the raw id_token. Use a server-side session or smaller opaque session cookie. If needed, also raise Nginx proxy_buffer_size/proxy_buffers, but the real fix is reducing response header size.

input:

2026-03-21 09:51:41,014 ERROR [http-nio-8080-exec-17] c.a.r.ReportingController - failed generating report reportId=77112 tenant=globex
java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space
	at java.base/java.util.Arrays.copyOf(Arrays.java:3537)
	at java.base/java.io.ByteArrayOutputStream.ensureCapacity(ByteArrayOutputStream.java:100)
	at java.base/java.io.ByteArrayOutputStream.write(ByteArrayOutputStream.java:130)
	at org.apache.poi.xssf.usermodel.XSSFWorkbook.write(XSSFWorkbook.java:1812)
	at com.acme.reporting.export.ExcelExporter.toBytes(ExcelExporter.java:88)
	at com.acme.reporting.service.ReportService.generateExcel(ReportService.java:141)
	at com.acme.reporting.controller.ReportingController.download(ReportingController.java:63)
2026-03-21 09:51:41,019 WARN  [http-nio-8080-exec-17] o.s.w.s.m.m.a.ExceptionHandlerExceptionResolver - Resolved [java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space]
2026-03-21 09:51:41,023 INFO  [http-nio-8080-exec-17] c.a.i.RequestLoggingFilter - completed status=500 path=/reports/77112/export.xlsx durationMs=28113 respBytes=92

output.issue: Java report export runs out of heap while building the Excel file in memory.
output.root_cause: The code writes the entire Apache POI workbook into a ByteArrayOutputStream, which loads the full XLSX into heap memory. For a large report, this exhausts the JVM heap and triggers OutOfMemoryError.
output.fix: Switch to Apache POI SXSSFWorkbook or another streaming writer, stream the response directly instead of buffering the whole file in a byte array, and cap export size. Increasing heap may reduce symptoms but does not fix the memory pattern.

	/app/cmd/consumer/main.go:77 +0x45

	/app/internal/repo/tx.go:31
main.(*Repo).MarkSettled(0xc00058aa80, {0x1304440, 0xc0008ea2d0}, {0xc0011941b0, 0xc}, 0x1388)
	/app/internal/repo/invoice.go:143 +0x1fd
2026-03-21T13:27:55.326Z INFO  consumer message failed offset=882190 will_retry=true

output.issue: Go Kafka consumer is using a transaction object after it has already been closed, causing retries and a nil-pointer panic.
output.root_cause: The settlement flow commits or rolls back the SQL transaction before Repo.MarkSettled finishes executing additional statements. Subsequent ExecContext calls run on an already closed tx wrapper, which surfaces as 'transaction has already been committed or rolled back' and then dereferences a nil tx context.
output.fix: Refactor processSettlement so all DB writes happen before commit/rollback and only one code path owns transaction finalization. Add guards in txCtx.ExecContext to return a clean error when tx is nil/closed, and cover duplicate-delivery handling with an idempotent transaction test.

output.issue: Python API is exhausting the SQLAlchemy connection pool and timing out on new DB requests.

output.issue: Java CSV import rejects prices because decimal values use a comma instead of the expected dot format.
output.root_cause: The importer deserializes the price field directly into BigDecimal using a parser that expects standard dot-decimal notation. The uploaded CSV contains locale-formatted values like "19,99".
output.fix: Normalize numeric fields based on the file locale before deserialization or configure the importer to parse locale-specific decimals. Also validate and reject mixed-format CSV files with a clear message before row processing starts.

output.issue: Go consumer is dropping valid-looking reservation events because the JSON field type does not match the struct definition.
output.root_cause: The producer sent quantity as a JSON string ("2") while the Go ReservationEvent struct expects an int. Unmarshal fails and the consumer treats it as a non-retryable bad payload.
output.fix: Make the producer send quantity as a numeric JSON value, or update the consumer to accept both string and integer representations with custom unmarshalling. Add schema validation at the event boundary so malformed payloads are rejected before publication.

[2026-03-21 07:41:19.816 +00:00 INF] job status=failed durationMs=294

output.issue: C# refund job cannot access S3 because the machine clock is significantly out of sync.
output.root_cause: AWS signed requests depend on accurate system time. The node reports ntp_sync=false and its UTC clock is about 14 minutes behind, causing S3 to reject the request with RequestTimeTooSkewed.
output.fix: Restore NTP time synchronization on the node or replace the unhealthy VM, then verify clock drift alarms are in place. Do not work around this in code because AWS request signing requires correct system time.

output.issue: PHP webhook idempotency logic is failing because the Redis key type does not match what the code expects.

output.fix: Migrate or delete legacy string idempotency keys before using the hash format, or version the key name so the new code writes to a different namespace like idem:v2:*. Add a type check and fallback cleanup for old keys.

output.issue: Rust translation service panics because the upstream response schema changed and deserialization no longer matches.

output.issue: Java service fails to start due to an incompatible HTTP client dependency mix on the classpath.
output.root_cause: Spring Cloud OpenFeign 4.2.1 is configuring HttpClient 5, but the application classpath only has Apache HttpClient 4.5.14 and is missing httpclient5. This causes NoSuchMethodError and ClassNotFoundException during startup.
output.fix: Align the dependency versions so OpenFeign uses the correct Apache HttpClient 5 artifacts, or explicitly disable that transport and use a supported alternative. Remove the older conflicting httpclient 4 dependency if it is being pulled transitively.

10.12.0.91 - - [21/Mar/2026:19:21:44 +0000] "GET /api/search?q=chair HTTP/1.1" 502 157 "[https://shop.internal/](https://shop.internal/)" "Mozilla/5.0"
2026-03-21T19:21:44.115Z INFO  search-api booting commit=9c17f22 node=v18.20.7
2026-03-21T19:21:44.181Z ERROR Error: ENOENT: no such file or directory, open '/app/config/search.synonyms.json'
    at Object.openSync (node:fs:596:3)
    at Object.readFileSync (node:fs:464:35)
    at loadSynonyms (/app/dist/search/config.js:19:28)
    at createSearchEngine (/app/dist/search/index.js:34:21)
    at bootstrap (/app/dist/server.js:27:18)
    at Object.<anonymous> (/app/dist/server.js:61:1)
    at Module._compile (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1364:14)
    at Module._extensions..js (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1422:10)
2026-03-21T19:21:44.182Z FATAL startup failed, exiting code=1 image=registry.internal/search-api:20260321.3

output.issue: Node.js search service is crash-looping on startup because a required config file is missing from the container image.
output.root_cause: The app tries to load /app/config/search.synonyms.json during bootstrap, but that file is not present in the deployed image or mounted volume. The process exits before binding port 3000, so Nginx gets connection refused.
output.fix: Include search.synonyms.json in the build artifact or mount it correctly at /app/config. Also make startup validate optional vs required config and fail deployment checks before traffic is routed to the pod.

output.issue: Invoice finalization is failing with a PostgreSQL deadlock.
output.root_cause: Two concurrent transactions are locking the same invoice and related ledger rows in different orders, causing circular lock dependency during commit.
output.fix: Make all code paths acquire locks in a consistent order, for example invoice row first then ledger rows, and add retry logic for deadlock-detected transactions. Consider SELECT ... FOR UPDATE on the invoice before updating dependent records.

input:
2026-03-21T10:52:01.201Z INFO  [gateway] reqId=4c963a1a-fb74-4753-9f9e-0bffeb2f6ab7 GET /api/files/01JYQX2VDS/download
2026-03-21T10:52:01.208Z INFO  [file-service] streaming object bucket=media-prod key=exports/2026/03/21/report-01JYQX2VDS.csv.gz range=full
2026-03-21T10:52:01.214Z ERROR [file-service] pipeline failed Error [ERR_STREAM_WRITE_AFTER_END]: write after end
    at new NodeError (node:internal/errors:405:5)
    at _write (node:internal/streams/writable:322:11)
    at Writable.write (node:internal/streams/writable:337:10)
    at Gunzip.ondata (node:internal/streams/readable:809:22)
    at Gunzip.emit (node:events:524:28)
    at addChunk (node:internal/streams/readable:561:12)
    at readableAddChunkPushByteMode (node:internal/streams/readable:512:3)
    at Readable.push (node:internal/streams/readable:392:5)
    at Zlib.processCallback (node:zlib:536:32)
2026-03-21T10:52:01.215Z WARN  [file-service] res.writableEnded=true headersSent=true reqAborted=false
2026-03-21T10:52:01.215Z DEBUG [file-service] code path: setHeader(Content-Length) -> res.end() on cache miss -> pipe(gunzip, res)
2026-03-21T10:52:01.216Z INFO  [gateway] status=502 upstream_reset=true duration_ms=15

output.issue: Node.js download endpoint is attempting to stream data after the HTTP response has already been closed.
output.root_cause: The handler calls res.end() on one branch before piping the gunzip stream into the same response object, so the stream writes to a finished response and crashes with write after end.
output.fix: Refactor the response path so only one code path owns the response lifecycle. Remove the premature res.end() call and return immediately after any early response branch. Add tests covering cache-miss streaming behavior.

output.issue: Go CSV importer aborts because one row has more columns than expected.

output.issue: PHP invoice preview generation fails because the process cannot create its temporary working directory.
output.root_cause: The app tries to write preview files to /tmp/pdfgen, but PHP-FPM is restricted by permissions or open_basedir and mkdir fails with Permission denied, so Imagick cannot read the expected image file.
output.fix: Configure a writable temp directory within the allowed filesystem paths, grant www-data permission to create it, and validate temp directory creation before starting PDF generation.

output.issue: C# refunds endpoint rejects the request because the amount field is not a valid decimal.
output.root_cause: The client sends amount as the string "12.34 USD" instead of a numeric decimal value expected by the API model.
output.fix: Send amount as a plain numeric field such as 12.34 and move the currency into a separate field if needed. Add request validation that returns a clear message when amount contains units or text.

2026-03-21T14:03:21.112302Z INFO worker: db statement_timeout=100ms

output.issue: Rust analytics sync job is crashing because its database query exceeds an extremely low PostgreSQL statement timeout.
output.root_cause: The session statement_timeout is set to 100ms, which is too low for the 90-day aggregation query, and the code unwraps the database error instead of handling it.
output.fix: Raise statement_timeout for this batch job or break the query into smaller chunks, and replace unwrap with proper error handling so timeout failures do not panic the worker.

output.issue: Node.js cart cleanup job is timing out while waiting on MySQL row locks.
output.root_cause: The bulk UPDATE is trying to lock many cart rows that are already locked by other transactions, and the long-running cleanup transaction hits MySQL lock wait timeout.
output.fix: Process carts in smaller batches with indexed selection, commit between batches, and add retry logic for lock timeouts. Consider selecting candidate IDs first and updating them in deterministic chunks to reduce contention.

output.issue: Java order publishing fails because the new Avro schema is incompatible with the registry's backward-compatibility rules.
output.root_cause: A new field, sales_channel, was added without a default value, so old messages cannot be read under BACKWARD compatibility and schema registration is rejected.
output.fix: Add a default value to the new field, for example null, and redeploy the schema. Keep new Avro fields backward-compatible by always providing defaults when compatibility is enforced.

output.issue: Go OIDC callback fails because the token response does not include an access_token.
output.root_cause: The provider is configured to return only an ID token for the requested flow or scope combination, but the client code assumes a standard OAuth token response that includes access_token.
output.fix: Align the OIDC client configuration with the provider's supported flow. Request the proper scopes and token response type so access_token is returned, or update the client logic if the integration is intended to be ID-token-only.

output.fix: Fix ownership and permissions on the exports directory so the application user can write files, and add a startup check for writable storage paths before jobs begin.

instruction: Analyze the following logs and identify the issue.

output.issue: Rails search endpoint fails because a non-numeric page parameter is passed directly into the Elasticsearch from offset.
output.root_cause: The controller or service is not validating page, so the string \"two\" is used where Elasticsearch expects an integer offset.
output.fix: Validate and coerce page to an integer before building the query, default invalid values to page 1, and return a 400 for malformed pagination parameters if strict input validation is desired.

output.issue: C# ERP sync cannot establish TLS because the remote certificate chain is incomplete or untrusted.
output.root_cause: The partner API presents a certificate chain that the client cannot build to a trusted root, resulting in PartialChain during SSL handshake.
output.fix: Install the missing intermediate/root CA in the client trust store or fix the partner server to send the full certificate chain. Do not bypass certificate validation in code.

output.issue: Rust notifier crashes because it inserts mail queue records with a hardcoded primary key value.
output.root_cause: The insert explicitly sends id=0 instead of using the database sequence or generated ID, so duplicate primary key violations occur immediately.
output.fix: Stop supplying a fixed id on insert. Let PostgreSQL generate the primary key via DEFAULT/serial/identity, or generate unique IDs in application code. Add an integration test for enqueue inserts.

output.issue: Node.js image worker cannot start the ImageMagick convert command because the binary is missing.

output.fix: Install ImageMagick in the worker image or switch the implementation to a bundled library like sharp. Add a startup dependency check so missing binaries fail fast before jobs are processed.

output.fix: Convert timestamps to MySQL DATETIME format before binding, or change the column and ORM mapping to a timezone-aware type that supports ISO 8601 serialization consistently.

output.issue: Node.js session refresh fails because tokens signed with the previous JWT secret are no longer accepted after rotation.
output.root_cause: The refresh token carries kid=20260320, but the service only loads the current secret 20260321 and has no grace period or fallback for verifying previously issued tokens.
output.fix: Load previous signing secrets during a rotation grace window and select the verification key by kid. Rotate secrets in a backward-compatible way so existing refresh tokens remain valid until expiry or planned revocation.

output.issue: Java file upload finalization fails when moving the temporary file to permanent storage.
output.root_cause: The code uses Files.move as an atomic rename across different filesystems, but /tmp/upload and /data/uploads are on separate devices, causing a Cross-device link error.
output.fix: Detect cross-filesystem moves and fall back to copy-plus-delete, or write temp files directly onto the target filesystem so the final move stays on the same device.

output.issue: Rails OAuth callback is rejected because the state stored in session is missing during the callback.

output.issue: C# inventory poller crashes while indexing upstream items by SKU because duplicate keys are present.

output.issue: Python sync job fails parsing the input because it expects a single JSON document but receives newline-delimited JSON records.

output.fix: Detect NDJSON input and parse it line by line, or require the provider to send a valid JSON array. Validate file format by content rather than relying on the .json extension.

output.issue: C# reporting query fails because SQL Server is asked to parse ISO timestamps using the wrong date format style.
output.root_cause: The query uses CONVERT(datetime, @param, 103), which expects dd/mm/yyyy-style strings, but the application passes ISO-8601 timestamps like 2026-03-21T00:00:00Z.
output.fix: Stop converting string dates inside SQL and pass strongly typed DateTime parameters directly. If string conversion is unavoidable, use a style compatible with ISO-8601.

output.issue: Order creation fails with database integrity error.
output.root_cause: A recent schema change made orders.created_at NOT NULL, but the SQLAlchemy model or insert path does not populate created_at and the database column has no default.
output.fix: Add a database default such as NOW() for orders.created_at and update the ORM model to set created_at automatically, then backfill any invalid migration state before redeploying.

output.issue: Session refresh endpoint crashes after Redis connection failure.
output.root_cause: The app assumes Redis returned a session object, but Redis is unreachable on 127.0.0.1:6379 and the code dereferences an undefined value without a null check.
output.fix: Point the Redis client to the correct host/port for the deployed environment and add defensive handling so refreshSession returns 401 or a retryable error when the session lookup fails instead of reading userId from undefined.

output.issue: Invoice worker panics with nil pointer after database timeout.

output.issue: JWT authentication fails because the signing key is too weak.

output.fix: Replace the JWT secret with a properly generated 256-bit or longer key, verify the environment variable is loaded intact in the deployment, and rotate tokens if the old weak secret was used in production.

output.issue: Go gRPC handler panics while decoding a dynamic payload field.

output.fix: Use a typed request struct or protobuf field for phone instead of map[string]interface{}, or validate and coerce the value safely before type asserting.

  Process 96 waits for ShareLock on transaction 902880; blocked by process 84.

output.issue: Checkout transaction is hitting a database deadlock.
output.root_cause: Concurrent code paths acquire locks in inconsistent order, with one transaction touching inventory then coupons while another touches coupons then inventory, causing circular wait.
output.fix: Standardize lock acquisition order across all checkout flows, keep transactions short, and retry deadlock-aborted transactions with idempotency protection.

output.issue: Kafka consumer stops because event timestamp format is incompatible with the schema.
output.root_cause: The producer sent createdAt as "dd-MM-yyyy HH:mm:ss" while the consumer expects an ISO-8601 value that Jackson can parse into java.time.Instant.
output.fix: Make the producer emit ISO-8601 UTC timestamps for createdAt, or update the consumer deserializer with an explicit formatter if that nonstandard format must be supported.

output.issue: OAuth callback fails with 502 due to oversized upstream response headers.
output.root_cause: The application sets too many or too-large cookies during OAuth callback, causing Nginx to reject the upstream response headers as too big.
output.fix: Reduce cookie size and count by storing session data server-side instead of in cookies, and only if necessary increase Nginx proxy buffer settings after shrinking the headers.

lastQuery=select * from products where lower(name) like '%wireless mouse%' order by created_at desc

output.issue: Search requests are timing out waiting for a database connection.
output.root_cause: The JDBC pool is exhausted, likely because expensive wildcard search queries are monopolizing all 20 connections and requests pile up behind them.
output.fix: Optimize the search query with proper indexing or full-text search, reduce connection hold time, inspect for leaked connections, and increase pool size only after fixing the slow query pattern.

output.issue: Kafka consumer loses group membership because message processing exceeds poll interval.
output.root_cause: A payment batch took 312221 ms to process, which is longer than max.poll.interval.ms=300000. Kafka rebalanced the consumer group before offset commit, so the commit failed.
output.fix: Reduce per-batch processing time, lower batch_size, move slow work off the poll loop, or increase max.poll.interval.ms to exceed worst-case processing time.

output.issue: Java application fails database connection during SSL handshake.
output.root_cause: The PostgreSQL server certificate is not trusted by the application's JVM truststore or the server presents a certificate chain the client cannot validate.
output.fix: Import the database CA or server certificate chain into the JVM truststore used by the app, or configure the correct sslrootcert/truststore settings that match the database certificate.

instruction: Analyze the following logs and identify the issue.
input:
2026-03-21T23:17:11.334Z INFO  webhook consumer started port=3000
2026-03-21T23:17:14.502Z ERROR StripeSignatureVerificationError: No signatures found matching the expected signature for payload.
    at validateComputedSignature (/app/node_modules/stripe/cjs/Webhooks.js:149:15)
    at Object.constructEvent (/app/node_modules/stripe/cjs/Webhooks.js:16:32)
    at /app/src/routes/webhooks/stripe.ts:27:26
rawBody? false content-type=application/json; charset=utf-8
note=express.json() mounted before webhook route

output.issue: Stripe webhook signature verification fails because the raw request body is unavailable.
output.root_cause: express.json() parses and mutates the request body before Stripe signature verification, but Stripe requires the original raw body bytes to validate the signature.
output.fix: Mount the Stripe webhook route before express.json() or use express.raw({ type: 'application/json' }) for that route so constructEvent receives the exact raw payload.

instruction: Analyze the following logs and identify the issue.

output.issue: Node process crashes from a memory leak under heavy WebSocket usage.

output.fix: Audit the WebSocket lifecycle so listeners are registered once and removed on disconnect, then load test again to confirm heap growth stabilizes before considering a higher memory limit.

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.11/site-packages/urllib3/connectionpool.py", line 537, in _make_request
    response = conn.getresponse()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.11/http/client.py", line 1390, in getresponse
    response.begin()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.11/http/client.py", line 325, in begin
    version, status, reason = self._read_status()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.11/socket.py", line 706, in readinto
    return self._sock.recv_into(b)
TimeoutError: timed out

During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred:
  File "/app/api/reporting.py", line 91, in summary
    r = requests.get("http://meta-service:9091/internal/accounts/4412", timeout=None)
requests.exceptions.ReadTimeout: HTTPConnectionPool(host='meta-service', port=9091): Read timed out. (read timeout=None)
2026-03-21T08:11:53.882Z INFO gunicorn.access 10.18.2.31 - - [21/Mar/2026:08:11:53 +0000] "GET /v1/reports/summary HTTP/1.1" 500 141 "-" "python-requests/2.31"

output.issue: Gunicorn worker is timing out while waiting on a blocking downstream HTTP call.
output.root_cause: The Python handler calls requests.get with timeout=None to meta-service, so a slow or hung downstream request blocks the worker until Gunicorn kills it.
output.fix: Set explicit connect and read timeouts on the outbound request, add retries/circuit breaking for meta-service, and move long-running aggregation off the request path if needed.

[2026-03-21T09:22:11.144Z] INFO ws-gateway starting version=3.7.2 node=v20.11.1

    at Receiver.startLoop (/usr/src/app/node_modules/ws/lib/receiver.js:155:16)

headers={"x-forwarded-for":"10.0.12.8","sec-websocket-extensions":"permessage-deflate","upgrade":"websocket"}
proxy=nginx/1.25.4 upstreamCompression=off appCompression=off

output.issue: WebSocket connections are failing due to a frame compression mismatch.
output.root_cause: The client negotiates permessage-deflate, but the proxy or app is not consistently handling compressed frames, so the Node ws server receives frames with RSV1 set unexpectedly.
output.fix: Align WebSocket compression settings end to end. Either disable permessage-deflate on both client and server/proxy or enable proper support consistently in the gateway and reverse proxy.

ts=2026-03-21T10:48:19.552Z lvl=error caller=http/decode.go:67 msg="json decode failed" route=/v1/events

output.issue: Go API rejects a request because a numeric field is being sent as a string.
output.root_cause: The request body provides retry_count as a JSON string, but the Go struct defines retry_count as an int, causing unmarshal failure.
output.fix: Send retry_count as a number in the client payload or update the server decoder/model to accept stringified integers and normalize them before validation.

[21-Mar-2026 12:05:11 UTC] PHP Fatal error:  Uncaught TypeError: hash_hmac(): Argument #2 ($data) must be of type string, array given in /var/www/app/src/Webhook/Verifier.php:44
Stack trace:

  thrown in /var/www/app/src/Webhook/Verifier.php on line 44
POST /webhooks/provider headers content-type=application/json body already parsed by middleware

output.issue: PHP webhook verification crashes because HMAC is being computed on an array instead of the raw payload string.
output.root_cause: The request body is parsed into an array before signature verification, but hash_hmac expects the original raw request body as a string.
output.fix: Read the raw request body for signature verification before JSON parsing, and only decode into an array after the HMAC check succeeds.

output.issue: ASP.NET API cannot parse the price field from the request body.
output.root_cause: The client sends price as the string "19,99" using a comma decimal separator, but System.Text.Json with invariant parsing expects a numeric JSON value or a dot-based decimal format.
output.fix: Send price as a proper JSON number like 19.99, or implement custom parsing/normalization for locale-formatted numeric strings before model binding.

output.issue: RabbitMQ consumer is acknowledging the same message more than once.
output.root_cause: The Go consumer calls Ack in the normal path and again in a deferred error handler, so RabbitMQ receives a duplicate ack for the same delivery tag.
output.fix: Ensure each delivery is acked or nacked exactly once by centralizing acknowledgment logic and removing the duplicate deferred Ack path.

output.issue: Session cleanup job is failing because it cannot acquire row locks in time.

output.issue: CSV import fails because the temp filesystem is out of space.

query=tenant:acme AND text:* size=10000 sort=created_at:desc

output.issue: Search requests are timing out because Elasticsearch is tripping the parent memory circuit breaker.
output.root_cause: The query requests a very large result set with expensive matching, pushing Elasticsearch heap usage above the parent circuit breaker limit and causing shard failures.
output.fix: Reduce result size, paginate properly, avoid broad wildcard queries, and tune the search pattern or cluster resources so requests stay within Elasticsearch heap limits.

output.issue: Go service is timing out gRPC requests before the downstream operation can reliably complete.
output.root_cause: The configured 5 second RPC deadline is too tight relative to the downstream latency profile, so the caller cancels the request before the handler finishes.
output.fix: Increase the gRPC timeout to match observed latency budgets, reduce downstream processing time where possible, and align retries so they do not pile onto requests already near deadline.

output.issue: Java payout workflow is failing due to a PostgreSQL deadlock between concurrent transactions.
output.root_cause: Two concurrent transaction paths acquire locks on payouts and ledger_entries in opposite order for the same payout, creating a circular wait and triggering deadlock detection.
output.fix: Standardize lock acquisition order across all payout code paths, keep transactions short, and add retry logic for deadlock-safe operations after fixing the ordering.

instruction: Analyze the following logs and identify the issue.

output.issue: Node.js cache reads fail because the client is talking to a Redis Cluster as if it were a standalone Redis instance.
output.root_cause: The service is configured with a single-node Redis client, but the server has Redis Cluster enabled and returns MOVED redirections that the client does not follow.
output.fix: Use a Redis Cluster-aware client configuration with the cluster seed nodes, or point the service at a non-cluster endpoint if standalone mode is intended.

org.springframework.orm.ObjectOptimisticLockingFailureException: Row was updated or deleted by another transaction (or unsaved-value mapping was incorrect): [com.acme.billing.Invoice#inv_8821]
	at org.springframework.orm.jpa.vendor.HibernateJpaDialect.convertHibernateAccessException(HibernateJpaDialect.java:325)
	at org.springframework.orm.jpa.vendor.HibernateJpaDialect.translateExceptionIfPossible(HibernateJpaDialect.java:241)
Caused by: org.hibernate.StaleObjectStateException: Row was updated or deleted by another transaction (or unsaved-value mapping was incorrect): [com.acme.billing.Invoice#inv_8821]
	at org.hibernate.event.internal.DefaultMergeEventListener.entityIsDetached(DefaultMergeEventListener.java:426)
	at org.hibernate.event.internal.DefaultMergeEventListener.merge(DefaultMergeEventListener.java:214)
2026-03-21 12:08:11.332 DEBUG 1 --- [nio-8080-exec-2] c.a.billing.InvoiceUpdateService         : payload version=7 dbVersion=8 status=paid
2026-03-21 12:08:11.333 WARN  1 --- [nio-8080-exec-2] c.a.billing.InvoiceUpdateService         : stale update from admin-ui tab openedAt=2026-03-21T11:55:03Z

output.issue: Java invoice update is rejected due to optimistic locking on a stale entity version.
output.root_cause: The admin UI submitted version 7 of the invoice after another transaction had already updated it to version 8, so Hibernate correctly prevented the stale overwrite.
output.fix: Handle optimistic lock conflicts by reloading the current record before save and prompting the user to retry or merge changes. Keep the version field in the update contract.

output.issue: Go consumer cannot decode messages because it is using a Protobuf decoder on JSON payloads.

output.fix: Align the topic contract so both producer and consumer use the same serialization format, or route JSON events to the correct topic and versioned consumer.

When attaching existing entities, ensure that only one entity instance with a given key value is attached.
   at Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.ChangeTracking.Internal.IdentityMap`1.ThrowIdentityConflict(InternalEntityEntry entry)

output.issue: C# avatar upsert fails because Entity Framework is tracking two different instances with the same primary key.

output.fix: Update the already tracked entity, or query with AsNoTracking if you intend to attach a new instance. Avoid mapping DTOs to duplicate tracked entities with the same key.

output.issue: Rust ingest worker crashes after exhausting the process file descriptor limit.
output.root_cause: The worker opens thousands of chunk files in one batch and is not closing them promptly, eventually hitting the nofile limit and failing with EMFILE.
output.fix: Close file handles as soon as each chunk is processed, reduce concurrent open files, and raise the OS nofile limit only after fixing the leak or excessive fan-out.

output.issue: Node.js OAuth code exchange fails because the redirect URI used in the token request does not exactly match the registered redirect URI.
output.root_cause: The callback URI sent during token exchange includes a trailing slash, while the registered redirect URI does not, causing the provider to reject the grant.
output.fix: Use the exact same redirect URI string in both the OAuth app registration and the token exchange code, including trailing slash behavior.

2026-03-21T12:47:29.519Z DEBUG expectedUrls=50000 actualUrls=50001 chunkSize=50000 remaining=-1
2026-03-21T12:47:29.520Z WARN  off-by-one suspected partition=4 indexStart=200000 indexEnd=250001
2026-03-21T12:47:29.520Z INFO  [worker] status=failed

output.issue: Node.js sitemap generation crashes because partition size calculation goes negative.
output.root_cause: An off-by-one bug in the partition end index produces remaining=-1, and that invalid value is passed into Buffer.alloc.
output.fix: Correct the partition boundary math so end indexes are exclusive and remaining counts never go negative. Add tests around exact boundary cases like 50,000 and 50,001 URLs.

output.fix: Set explicit connect/read timeouts on the outbound request, add retries with backoff, and split large reconcile batches so work completes within the Celery task time budget.

2026-03-21T23:09:12.613Z WARN  dropping webhook from partner=ledgerx reason=auth_failed request_id=6e6bf7f7c9f1
2026-03-22T01:22:31+02:00 systemd-timesyncd[421]: Initial synchronization to time server 169.254.169.123:123 (Amazon Time Sync Service).

output.issue: Webhook authentication is failing because JWTs are considered expired.
output.root_cause: The server clock was out of sync until NTP corrected it, so token validation used incorrect system time and rejected otherwise valid partner JWTs.
output.fix: Ensure time synchronization starts before the service, monitor clock drift, and allow a small JWT clock-skew tolerance during validation.

instruction: Analyze the following logs and identify the issue.
input:
2026-03-21 18:37:59.017 ERROR [main] liquibase.changelog : Change Set db/changelog/20260321-add-status-index.xml::20260321-1::ops failed.  Error: ERROR: could not create unique index "idx_users_email_unique"
  Detail: Key (email)=([alex@northpeak.io](mailto:alex@northpeak.io)) is duplicated. [Failed SQL: (0) CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_users_email_unique ON users(email)]
2026-03-21 18:37:59.018 ERROR [main] o.s.boot.SpringApplication : Application run failed
liquibase.exception.MigrationFailedException: Migration failed for changeset db/changelog/20260321-add-status-index.xml::20260321-1::ops:
     Reason: liquibase.exception.DatabaseException: ERROR: could not create unique index "idx_users_email_unique"
  Detail: Key (email)=([alex@northpeak.io](mailto:alex@northpeak.io)) is duplicated.

output.issue: Startup migration fails because a new unique index cannot be created on duplicate email data.
output.root_cause: Existing rows in users already contain duplicate email values, so Liquibase cannot apply the unique index migration.
output.fix: Identify and clean up duplicate user emails before applying the migration, or write a staged migration that deduplicates data and only then adds the unique index.

Mar 21 20:03:14 worker-1 node[41]:     at new NodeError (node:internal/errors:405:5)

output.issue: Node.js webhook handler is trying to send more than one HTTP response.
output.root_cause: One branch sends a response during signature verification, but the function does not return, so later code attempts to send headers again.
output.fix: Return immediately after every res.send/res.json/res.status call, and refactor the webhook handler so only one response path can execute.

output.fix: Deploy the updated JWT secret or key set to this service, support key rotation by kid, and verify all consumers refresh secrets in sync with the issuer.

instruction: Analyze the following logs and identify the issue.

output.issue: Go API rejects profile updates because the phone number overflows the expected integer type.

instruction: Analyze the following logs and identify the issue.

output.issue: Rails background job keeps executing SQL inside a failed transaction.

output.fix: Handle the duplicate run creation explicitly, roll back the transaction immediately after the first SQL error, and make the export run creation idempotent for retries.

output.issue: Login requests fail because Django is trying to use a closed PostgreSQL connection.
output.root_cause: Gunicorn workers inherited a stale database connection after preload or long idle time, and the app reused it instead of reopening before the query.
output.fix: Disable unsafe DB connection reuse across forks by avoiding shared connections with Gunicorn preload, enable Django connection health checks or shorter CONN_MAX_AGE, and ensure each worker opens its own fresh PostgreSQL connection after fork.

output.issue: Cache warmer crashes due to unsynchronized concurrent writes to a Go map.
output.root_cause: Multiple goroutines are writing to the same native map without a mutex, sync.Map, or single-writer coordination.
output.fix: Protect the shared map with a sync.Mutex or replace it with sync.Map, and review WarmJob concurrency so all writes to WarmIndex go through a thread-safe path.

output.issue: Celery worker cannot start consuming because the queue declaration conflicts with the existing RabbitMQ queue settings.

output.fix: Make the Celery queue declaration match the existing RabbitMQ queue arguments exactly, or delete and recreate the queue during a coordinated deployment if the topology intentionally changed.

output.issue: BullMQ jobs are stalling and failing completion because the worker loses its Redis lock.
output.root_cause: The Node.js event loop is blocked for tens of seconds, so the worker cannot renew the job lock before BullMQ tries to finish the job.
output.fix: Find and remove the long blocking synchronous work in the email worker, move heavy processing off the event loop, and tune BullMQ lock duration only after the blocking code path is fixed.

output.issue: Python API is exhausting the SQLAlchemy database connection pool.

output.fix: Ensure every request closes or returns its SQLAlchemy session promptly, stream large exports instead of holding connections during long work, and only increase pool size after verifying there are no session leaks.

instruction: Analyze the following logs and identify the issue.
input: ts=2026-03-21T08:12:49.118Z level=error caller=grpc/server.go:88 msg="rpc failed" method=/files.FileService/UploadChunk req_id=7b92a31c err="rpc error: code = ResourceExhausted desc = grpc: received message larger than max (9438208 vs. 4194304)"\npeer=10.3.4.8:54118 chunk_size=9438208 max_recv=4194304

output.issue: Go gRPC server is rejecting uploads because the request message is too large.
output.root_cause: The client sends a 9.4 MB chunk, but the server's maximum receive message size is configured to 4 MB.
output.fix: Reduce client chunk size below the server limit or increase the gRPC max receive message size on the server and client if larger payloads are intentional.

fd_soft_limit=1024 active_open_files=1021 temp_handles=87

output.issue: Rust service is hitting the operating system file descriptor limit.
output.root_cause: The process opens too many files concurrently and then unwraps the failing file-open result, triggering a panic once the per-process FD limit is reached.
output.fix: Close file handles promptly, limit concurrent file operations, replace unwrap with proper error handling, and raise the process ulimit only after reducing descriptor leaks or bursts.

2026-03-21 11:31:49.114 ERROR [main] c.a.startup.App : startup failed

-> Resolved locally    : 947721155
Either revert the changes to the migration, or run repair to update the schema history.
app_version=20260321.4 db=postgresql://db:5432/app

output.issue: Flyway refuses startup because a previously applied migration was modified.
output.root_cause: Migration version 42 was edited after it had already been applied to the database, so the local checksum no longer matches the stored schema history checksum.
output.fix: Do not edit applied Flyway migrations. Revert the file to its original content and add a new migration for changes, or run flyway repair only if you have verified the database state intentionally matches the modified script.

output.issue: Withdrawal job fails due to MySQL deadlock during wallet update
output.root_cause: Concurrent transactions are updating wallet-related rows in inconsistent lock order, causing InnoDB to detect a deadlock and abort one transaction.
output.fix: Enforce consistent row locking order across payout flows, wrap the transaction in retry logic for deadlocks, and narrow the transaction scope to reduce lock contention.

output.issue: Node.js signup flow hits duplicate email unique constraint
output.root_cause: Two concurrent signup requests for the same email passed the pre-check and one later failed at insert time due to a race condition on the unique email index.
output.fix: Treat the database unique index as the source of truth, catch the Prisma unique-constraint error and return a 409, and remove reliance on a separate read-before-write existence check.

output.issue: PHP app cannot connect to MySQL because localhost resolves to a local socket path
output.root_cause: PDO is using host=localhost inside a container, which attempts a Unix socket connection that does not exist instead of connecting to the MySQL service over TCP.

output.fix: Update the consumer schema to match the produced message format or add backward-compatible field aliases, and replace unwrap with proper error handling plus dead-letter routing.

output.issue: OAuth callback fails because upstream response headers are too large for Nginx
output.root_cause: The Node.js auth service returns an oversized Set-Cookie header around 11 KB, exceeding Nginx's configured proxy header buffer limits.
output.fix: Reduce cookie size by storing session data server-side or splitting/compressing it, and increase Nginx proxy buffer settings only if necessary as a secondary measure.

output.issue: PostgreSQL migration fails because concurrent index build exceeds statement timeout
output.root_cause: The CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY operation on a very large events table takes longer than the configured 30-second statement_timeout, so PostgreSQL cancels it.
output.fix: Run the index migration with a higher or disabled statement_timeout for that session, ideally during low-traffic periods, and monitor long-running concurrent index creation separately.

output.root_cause: After the base image change, the JVM truststore no longer trusts the SMTP server certificate chain, causing TLS handshake validation to fail.
output.fix: Import the SMTP server's issuing CA certificate into the runtime truststore or switch to a base image that includes the required CA chain, then verify TLS against email.internal.local:465.

output.issue: Laravel cannot dispatch the queued job because the payload is not serializable.
output.root_cause: The SyncCatalog job includes a Closure in one of its properties or constructor arguments, and Laravel queues must serialize the job payload.
output.fix: Remove Closure or service objects from the queued job payload and pass only primitive data or IDs, then resolve dependencies inside the job handler at execution time.

output.issue: ASP.NET Core host fails to start due to invalid service lifetime wiring.
output.root_cause: A singleton background worker is directly injecting AppDbContext, which is registered as scoped and only valid within a request or explicitly created scope.
output.fix: Inject IServiceScopeFactory into the background worker and create a scope per execution, or refactor the dependency graph so scoped services are not injected into singletons.

output.issue: The Go worker crashes due to unsafe concurrent access to a shared map.

output.issue: Legacy post import fails because MySQL rejects a zero-date timestamp.
output.root_cause: The importer still writes 0000-00-00 00:00:00 for missing publish dates, but MySQL strict mode now rejects that invalid datetime value.
output.fix: Store NULL for unknown timestamps instead of zero dates and update the import mapping and column nullability rules accordingly.

output.issue: User creation fails with duplicate email constraint violation
output.root_cause: Incoming email values are not normalized before insert, so trailing whitespace and casing differences bypass app-level checks but still collide with the database unique index on the canonical email value already stored.
output.fix: Trim and lowercase email values before validation and insert, add a deterministic normalization step in the API layer, and return a 409 conflict instead of a 500 when Prisma throws P2002.

output.issue: BullMQ queue operations fail because a Redis key has the wrong type

output.fix: Delete or rename the conflicting Redis key after confirming it is safe, isolate BullMQ into its own key prefix or Redis database, and prevent application code from reusing queue-managed keys.

output.issue: Checkout transaction fails due to PostgreSQL deadlock
output.root_cause: Two concurrent code paths acquire row locks in opposite order, one updating inventory then orders while another updates orders then inventory, creating a circular wait.
output.fix: Standardize lock acquisition order across all transactional code paths, keep transactions short, and add safe retry logic for deadlock-detected failures.

output.issue: OAuth callback returns 502 because upstream response headers are too large
output.root_cause: The application is setting oversized session cookies, causing the upstream response header size to exceed Nginx buffer limits.
output.fix: Reduce session payload stored in cookies, move session state server-side or into a compact token, and increase Nginx proxy buffer settings only as a secondary mitigation.

output.issue: Node.js user lookup fails because a cached prepared statement name no longer matches the current SQL parameter list.

output.issue: Java session loading fails because cached session objects deserialize as generic maps instead of UserSession objects.
output.root_cause: The Redis serializer configuration changed to JSON without preserving type metadata, so older or newly written cache entries deserialize into LinkedHashMap and the service then casts them directly to UserSession.
output.fix: Use a typed serializer for UserSession or enable safe type metadata, and version or flush session cache keys during serializer migrations so incompatible cached objects are not reused.

Note that it's not possible to mix DateTimes with different Kinds in an array/range. See the Npgsql documentation for more details.
   at Npgsql.Internal.TypeHandlers.DateTimeHandlers.TimestampTzHandler.ValidateAndGetLength(DateTimeOffset value, NpgsqlParameter parameter)

output.issue: C# ledger entry creation fails because a non-UTC DateTimeOffset is being written to a PostgreSQL timestamptz column.
output.root_cause: The code uses DateTimeOffset.Now with a local +05:30 offset, but Npgsql requires UTC-normalized offsets for timestamp with time zone writes in this mapping path.
output.fix: Normalize timestamps to UTC before persisting, for example by using DateTimeOffset.UtcNow or converting local offsets to UTC explicitly before assigning the database parameter.

output.issue: Node.js JWT signing fails because the configured private key is not in a usable PEM format.
output.root_cause: The environment variable contains a one-line encoded key without PEM headers and line formatting, so Node's crypto decoder cannot parse it for RS256 signing.
output.fix: Store the private key in proper PEM format or base64-decode it before use, and validate key parsing during startup instead of on the first signing request.

output.issue: Go upload client fails because the declared Content-Length does not match the actual request body length.

output.fix: Do not set Content-Length manually when streaming compressed bodies, or compute it after compression. Let the Go HTTP client handle chunked transfer if the final size is not known upfront.

output.issue: Python metrics pull fails because it is trying to use HTTPS against a plain HTTP endpoint.

output.issue: Go multipart upload completion fails because the ETag sent for a part does not exactly match the uploaded part's ETag.
output.root_cause: A custom wrapper strips the quote characters from the UploadPart ETag before CompleteMultipartUpload, so S3 rejects the completion request with InvalidPart.
output.fix: Pass the exact ETag returned by UploadPart into CompleteMultipartUpload, preferably by using the SDK's structured response objects instead of manual string normalization.

  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.11/subprocess.py", line 1024, in **init**
    self._execute_child(args, executable, preexec_fn, close_fds,

output.issue: Python export worker cannot generate PDFs because the external binary is missing
output.root_cause: The worker image was built from a slim base image without installing wkhtmltopdf, but the task still invokes it as a required runtime dependency.
output.fix: Install wkhtmltopdf in the worker image or switch the export pipeline to a bundled PDF renderer, then add a startup health check that verifies required binaries are present.

output.issue: Go service fails when writing a complex map directly to Redis
output.root_cause: The code passes a map[string]interface{} value into go-redis without serializing it first, but the Redis client cannot marshal arbitrary maps automatically.
output.fix: Serialize structured cache payloads to JSON or another stable binary format before Set, and standardize the cache layer so only supported primitive or encoded values are written to Redis.

output.issue: Go WebSocket endpoint rejects valid browser upgrade requests
output.root_cause: The Gorilla WebSocket upgrader CheckOrigin policy is too strict or still configured for old frontend domains, so the current admin origin is blocked.
output.fix: Update CheckOrigin to explicitly allow the current trusted frontend origin list and keep the validation strict rather than disabling origin checks entirely.

output.root_cause: The producer sends lowercase snake_case enum values like in_progress, but the consumer expects uppercase enum constants such as IN_PROGRESS without a custom deserializer or mapping.
output.fix: Standardize enum serialization across services or add a Jackson enum deserializer that maps producer values to the consumer enum constants, then update contract tests to enforce the agreed format.

output.issue: Kotlin sync requests time out because blocking database/network work is running on the default coroutine dispatcher
output.root_cause: The service executes blocking JDBC and socket operations on DefaultDispatcher instead of an IO dispatcher, starving coroutine threads and cascading into timeouts and closed connections.
output.fix: Move blocking database and network calls into withContext(Dispatchers.IO) or a dedicated executor, then load-test to confirm dispatcher starvation is gone.

remote=[https://sms-gateway.legacycorp.local:443](https://sms-gateway.legacycorp.local:443) runtime=.NET 8.0 image=2026.03.21-2

output.issue: SMS integration fails during TLS handshake because the client and server do not support the same protocol version
output.root_cause: The legacy SMS gateway only supports outdated TLS versions, while the .NET 8 client negotiates newer protocol requirements and the server rejects the handshake with ProtocolVersion.
output.fix: Upgrade or reconfigure the SMS gateway to support modern TLS, or route through a supported intermediary service instead of weakening the client security defaults.

output.issue: Scheduled job cannot acquire a PostgreSQL advisory lock because it calls the function with the wrong argument type

output.fix: Hash the string lock name to a bigint or split it into two int4 values before calling pg_try_advisory_lock with the correct signature.

instruction: Analyze the following logs and identify the issue.
input:
2026-03-21 16:03:18,331 INFO  [gunicorn.error] worker=pid:31 handling POST /v1/import/users
2026-03-21 16:03:18,349 ERROR [app.importer] import failed
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/srv/app/app/services/user_import.py", line 48, in import_csv
    reader = csv.DictReader(io.StringIO(body.decode("utf-8")))
UnicodeDecodeError: 'utf-8' codec can't decode byte 0xff in position 0: invalid start byte
upload_filename=users_march.csv content_type=text/csv first_bytes=b'\xff\xfeN\x00a\x00m\x00e\x00'
status=500

output.issue: User import fails because the uploaded CSV is not encoded as UTF-8.
output.root_cause: The uploaded file appears to be UTF-16 encoded with a BOM, but the importer unconditionally decodes the payload as UTF-8.
output.fix: Detect and handle BOM or file encoding before parsing, or normalize uploads to UTF-8 during ingestion with a clear validation error when unsupported encodings are provided.

instruction: Analyze the following logs and identify the issue.
input:
2026-03-21T16:39:22.551Z INFO  [postgres] applying migration 202603211635_add_idx_users_lower_email
2026-03-21T16:39:22.714Z ERROR [postgres] ERROR:  could not create unique index "idx_users_lower_email_unique"
DETAIL:  Key (lower(email::text))=([sam@example.com](mailto:sam@example.com)) is duplicated.
STATEMENT: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX CONCURRENTLY idx_users_lower_email_unique ON users (lower(email));
2026-03-21T16:39:22.715Z FATAL migration runner failed

output.issue: Unique email index creation fails because duplicate case-insensitive emails already exist.
output.root_cause: The users table contains multiple rows whose emails differ only by case, so creating a unique index on lower(email) is rejected.
output.fix: Identify and resolve duplicate case-insensitive email records before creating the unique index, then enforce normalized email writes going forward.

instruction: Analyze the following logs and identify the issue.
input:
2026-03-21 17:14:47.119  INFO 1 --- [task-scheduler-1] c.a.jobs.WebhookDrainJob                 : draining webhook backlog
2026-03-21 17:14:47.141 ERROR 1 --- [task-scheduler-1] c.a.jobs.WebhookDrainJob                 : cannot parse callback url
java.net.URISyntaxException: Illegal character in authority at index 8: https://api partner.example.com/callback
	at java.base/java.net.URI$Parser.fail(URI.java:2976)
	at java.base/java.net.URI$Parser.parseAuthority(URI.java:3324)
rowId=99122 callback_url="https://api partner.example.com/callback"

output.issue: Webhook drain job fails because a stored callback URL is invalid.
output.root_cause: The callback_url contains an unescaped space in the authority portion of the URL, making it syntactically invalid for Java URI parsing.
output.fix: Reject malformed callback URLs at write time with stricter validation, and clean or correct the invalid stored URL before retrying the drain job.

instruction: Analyze the following logs and identify the issue.
input:
2026-03-21T17:55:11.003Z INFO  [api] GET /v1/metrics/usage?from=2026-03-01&to=2026-03-21 req_id=fe2a91d1
2026-03-21T17:55:41.008Z ERROR [typeorm] QueryFailedError: canceling statement due to lock timeout
    at PostgresQueryRunner.query (/usr/src/app/node_modules/typeorm/driver/postgres/PostgresQueryRunner.js:211:19)
query: SELECT date_trunc('day', created_at) d, count(*) FROM api_requests WHERE created_at >= $1 AND created_at < $2 GROUP BY d ORDER BY d
parameters: ["2026-03-01T00:00:00.000Z","2026-03-22T00:00:00.000Z"]
blocked_by=pid 22194 statement='ALTER TABLE api_requests ADD COLUMN trace_id text'
2026-03-21T17:55:41.009Z WARN  [api] usage metrics query aborted after waiting on lock

output.issue: Usage metrics endpoint fails because the query is blocked by a schema lock.
output.root_cause: A concurrent ALTER TABLE on api_requests holds a lock that blocks the reporting SELECT long enough to hit the lock timeout.
output.fix: Schedule blocking schema changes during low-traffic windows, use safer online migration patterns, and keep reporting queries away from tables undergoing intrusive DDL when possible.

instruction: Analyze the following logs and identify the issue.
input:
2026-03-21T18:27:34.118Z INFO  [python.worker] job=send_campaign id=cmp_9912
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/srv/app/app/tasks/campaigns.py", line 57, in send_campaign
    recipients = json.loads(row["recipient_ids"])
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.11/json/**init**.py", line 346, in loads
    return _default_decoder.decode(s)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.11/json/decoder.py", line 337, in decode
    obj, end = self.raw_decode(s, idx=_w(s, 0).end())
json.decoder.JSONDecodeError: Expecting property name enclosed in double quotes: line 1 column 2 (char 1)
row={"campaign_id":"cmp_9912","recipient_ids":"['usr_1','usr_2']"}

output.issue: Campaign job fails because recipient_ids is stored in invalid JSON format.
output.root_cause: The database row contains a Python-style list string with single quotes instead of valid JSON, so json.loads cannot parse it.
output.fix: Store recipient_ids as real JSON or a proper array type, backfill malformed rows, and validate serialization at write time so Python repr strings are never persisted as JSON payloads.

instruction: Analyze the following logs and identify the issue.
input:
2026-03-21T19:01:28.771Z INFO  [node-api] POST /v1/checkout/confirm req_id=11b8d0c2
2026-03-21T19:01:28.779Z ERROR [stripe] StripeInvalidRequestError: No such payment_method: 'pm_991882'
    at res.toJSON.then.StripeAPIError.message (/usr/src/app/node_modules/stripe/cjs/Error.js:11:20)
    at process.processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:95:5)
raw: { code: 'resource_missing', doc_url: '[https://stripe.com/docs/error-codes/resource-missing](https://stripe.com/docs/error-codes/resource-missing)', message: "No such payment_method: 'pm_991882'", param: 'payment_method', request_log_url: '[https://dashboard.stripe.com/test/logs/req_xxx](https://dashboard.stripe.com/test/logs/req_xxx)' }
account_mode=live frontend_mode=test customer=cus_live_882

output.issue: Checkout confirmation fails because the payment method does not exist in the current Stripe mode.
output.root_cause: The backend is operating in live mode while the provided payment method was likely created in test mode, so Stripe cannot find that resource in the live account namespace.
output.fix: Use matching Stripe environments end to end so frontend-generated payment method IDs and backend API keys are both test or both live, and block cross-mode identifiers before confirm calls.

instruction: Analyze the following logs and identify the issue.
input:
2026-03-21T05:18:02.441Z INFO  api request_id=64c92b91 method=POST path=/v1/orders
2026-03-21T05:18:02.517Z ERROR psycopg.errors.SerializationFailure: could not serialize access due to concurrent update
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/app/services/order_service.py", line 88, in create_order
    session.commit()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/sqlalchemy/orm/session.py", line 2032, in commit
    trans.commit(_to_root=True)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/sqlalchemy/engine/default.py", line 951, in do_execute
    cursor.execute(statement, parameters)
psycopg.errors.SerializationFailure: could not serialize access due to concurrent update
DETAIL:  Reason code: Canceled on identification as a pivot, during write.
CONTEXT:  SQL statement "UPDATE inventory SET reserved = reserved + $1 WHERE sku = $2"
isolation=SERIALIZABLE sku=SKU-991 qty=1 retry_count=0
2026-03-21T05:18:02.518Z INFO  response status=500 duration_ms=76

output.issue: Order creation fails under concurrent traffic because the transaction is aborted by PostgreSQL serialization rules.
output.root_cause: The service runs inventory updates in SERIALIZABLE isolation, and concurrent transactions on the same SKU trigger a serialization failure that is not retried.
output.fix: Add retry logic for serialization failures around the whole transaction, or reduce contention by using row-level locking or a lower isolation level only if business rules still hold.

instruction: Analyze the following logs and identify the issue.
input:
[2026-03-21T06:07:14.103Z] INFO  queue-worker booted name=thumbnail-events node=v20.11.1
[2026-03-21T06:07:14.444Z] ERROR AMQP connection closed
Error: Channel closed by server: 406 (PRECONDITION-FAILED) with message "PRECONDITION_FAILED - inequivalent arg 'x-dead-letter-exchange' for queue 'media.jobs' in vhost '/': received 'media.dlx.v2' but current is 'media.dlx'"
    at C.accept (/usr/src/app/node_modules/amqplib/lib/channel.js:324:19)
    at Connection.mainAccept [as accept] (/usr/src/app/node_modules/amqplib/lib/connection.js:579:33)
    at Socket.go (/usr/src/app/node_modules/amqplib/lib/connection.js:431:16)
consumer_tag=amq.ctag-wkP1e queue=media.jobs durable=true deploy=20260321.3

output.issue: RabbitMQ consumer cannot start because the declared queue arguments do not match the existing queue.
output.root_cause: The Node worker redeclares media.jobs with a different dead-letter exchange than the one already stored on the broker, so RabbitMQ rejects the channel.
output.fix: Use the exact same queue arguments as the existing queue, or delete and recreate the queue during a controlled migration if changing dead-letter configuration is intentional.

instruction: Analyze the following logs and identify the issue.
input:
2026-03-21 06:42:29.881 ERROR [main] o.s.b.SpringApplication : Application run failed
org.springframework.beans.factory.BeanCreationException: Error creating bean with name 'paymentClient' defined in class path resource [com/acme/config/FeignConfig.class]: Invocation of init method failed; nested exception is java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Could not resolve placeholder 'payment.api.base-url' in value "${payment.api.base-url}"
	at org.springframework.beans.factory.config.PlaceholderConfigurerSupport.doProcessProperties(PlaceholderConfigurerSupport.java:230)
activeProfiles=[prod] env_source=Kubernetes ConfigMap=payments-api-config missing_key=payment.api.base-url

output.issue: Spring Boot fails during startup because a required configuration property is missing.
output.root_cause: The application expects payment.api.base-url, but that property is absent from the active environment configuration in prod.
output.fix: Add payment.api.base-url to the deployed ConfigMap or environment variables, and fail configuration validation earlier for required properties.

instruction: Analyze the following logs and identify the issue.
input: ts=2026-03-21T07:15:07.119Z level=error caller=handler/user.go:73 msg="request failed" route=/v1/users/991/roles req_id=1aa3ef8d err="sql: Scan error on column index 2, name \"roles\": unsupported Scan, storing driver.Value type []uint8 into type *[]string"\nquery="SELECT id,email,roles FROM users WHERE id = ?" args=[991]\ndb=mysql8 roles_column_type=json

output.issue: Go API cannot read the roles column because the result type does not match the scan destination.
output.root_cause: The MySQL roles column is JSON and comes back as raw bytes, but the code tries to scan it directly into *[]string without unmarshalling.
output.fix: Scan the JSON column into []byte or json.RawMessage first, then unmarshal into []string, or implement a custom Scanner type for roles.

instruction: Analyze the following logs and identify the issue.
input:
I, [2026-03-21T07:48:12.901032 #1]  INFO -- : [ActiveJob] [WebhookDeliveryJob] Performing WebhookDeliveryJob (Job ID: 8a5c3b9f-2c6e-4a2b-bf9b-fc95d1d8332e)
E, [2026-03-21T07:48:13.012522 #1] ERROR -- : [ActiveJob] [WebhookDeliveryJob] Error performing WebhookDeliveryJob (Job ID: 8a5c3b9f-2c6e-4a2b-bf9b-fc95d1d8332e) from Sidekiq(default) in 91.7ms: OpenSSL::SSL::SSLError (SSL_connect returned=1 errno=0 peeraddr=54.22.19.8:443 state=error: tlsv1 alert protocol version):
/app/lib/webhook_client.rb:31:in `post'
endpoint=https://hooks.partner.io/events ruby=3.3.1 openssl=1.1.1 server_requires_tls>=1.2

output.issue: Ruby job cannot deliver the webhook because TLS negotiation fails.
output.root_cause: The client runtime is attempting an older TLS protocol version that the partner endpoint rejects because it requires TLS 1.2 or newer.
output.fix: Upgrade the Ruby/OpenSSL runtime or explicitly configure the HTTP client to use TLS 1.2+, then redeploy and verify the handshake against the partner endpoint.

instruction: Analyze the following logs and identify the issue.
input:
2026-03-21 08:11:55.667 +00:00 [ERR] Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Database.Command[20102]
Failed executing DbCommand (7ms) [Parameters=[@p0='?' (DbType = Guid), @p1='?' (DbType = DateTime)], CommandType='Text', CommandTimeout='30']
INSERT INTO audit_logs (id, created_at) VALUES (@p0, @p1);

output.issue: Audit logging insert fails because it references an invalid actor ID.
output.root_cause: The middleware is writing an audit_logs row with a zero UUID actor_id that does not exist in the referenced users table.
output.fix: Do not write placeholder actor IDs. Propagate the authenticated user ID correctly, allow NULL only if the schema supports anonymous actions, or create a dedicated system actor record if needed.

instruction: Analyze the following logs and identify the issue.
input:
[21-Mar-2026 08:37:40 UTC] PHP Warning:  finfo_file(/var/www/storage/uploads/991/report.pdf): Failed to open stream: No such file or directory in /var/www/app/Service/AttachmentScanner.php on line 52
[21-Mar-2026 08:37:40 UTC] PHP Fatal error:  Uncaught RuntimeException: attachment missing after move_uploaded_file in /var/www/app/Service/AttachmentScanner.php:53
Stack trace:
#0 /var/www/app/Controller/UploadController.php(88): App\Service\AttachmentScanner->scan('/var/www/storage...')
#1 {main}
tmp_name=/tmp/phpV3Yx2B final_path=/var/www/storage/uploads/991/report.pdf move_uploaded_file=true container_mount=/var/www/storage readOnly=true

output.issue: PHP upload processing fails because the destination storage mount is not writable.
output.root_cause: The container mount backing /var/www/storage is read-only, so the uploaded file is not actually persisted even though the code proceeds to scan the expected final path.
output.fix: Mount the upload storage volume with write permissions for the PHP process, and verify move_uploaded_file succeeds before continuing to scan the file.

instruction: Analyze the following logs and identify the issue.
input:
2026-03-21T09:05:18.440Z WARN  grpc-server method=/auth.AuthService/RefreshToken trace_id=ae134d09
thread 'tokio-runtime-worker' panicked at src/auth/refresh.rs:112:37:
index out of bounds: the len is 1 but the index is 1
note: run with `RUST_BACKTRACE=1`environment variable to display a backtrace
header.authorization="Bearer"
peer=10.32.8.2:54419

output.issue: Rust auth service panics on malformed Authorization headers.
output.root_cause: The code splits the Authorization header and blindly accesses the second token part, but the request only contains the word Bearer with no actual token.
output.fix: Validate the Authorization header format before indexing into split results, and return a structured unauthenticated error for malformed bearer headers.

instruction: Analyze the following logs and identify the issue.
input:
2026-03-21T09:31:44.228Z INFO  [consumer-2] kafka listener started topic=shipment-updates group=tracking-sync
2026-03-21T09:31:44.511Z ERROR [consumer-2] com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.exc.InvalidTypeIdException: Could not resolve type id 'DELIVERD' as a subtype of`com.acme.events.ShipmentEvent`: known type ids = [CREATED, IN_TRANSIT, DELIVERED, RETURNED]
 at [Source: (String)"{"type":"DELIVERD","tracking_id":"trk_7712"}"; line: 1, column: 9]
handler=ShipmentEventHandler partition=1 offset=440912

output.issue: Java consumer rejects the event because the type discriminator value is invalid.
output.root_cause: The producer emits type=DELIVERD, which is a misspelled event type not recognized by the consumer's polymorphic deserialization config.
output.fix: Correct the producer to emit DELIVERED, and add schema validation or enum-based contract tests so invalid event type strings are caught before publish.

instruction: Analyze the following logs and identify the issue.
input:
[2026-03-21T10:02:01.091Z] INFO  api POST /v1/rebuild-index reqId=7cb4f72a
[2026-03-21T10:02:01.331Z] ERROR TypeError: Do not know how to serialize a BigInt
    at JSON.stringify (<anonymous>)
    at publishAudit (/usr/src/app/src/audit/publisher.js:28:19)
    at async rebuildIndex (/usr/src/app/src/routes/admin.js:114:5)
payloadPreview={"jobId":991122334455667788n,"actorId":441}
